title: T-122 — Per-org authorization policies for NPD post-release edits and Technical approval gates
prompt:
# T-122 — Per-org authorization policies for NPD post-release edits and Technical approval gates

## Goal
Implement Settings/Auth foundation for per-org authorization policies consumed by NPD post-release edit requests and Technical product-spec approval gates. This task does not implement NPD or Technical application workflows; it only creates Settings-owned policy storage, Server Actions, permission checks, seed defaults, and the SET-011b Authorization Policies screen.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/db/schema/settings-auth-policies.ts [create/modify]
- apps/web/db/migrations/*authorization_policies*.sql [create]
- apps/web/lib/rbac/permissions.enum.ts [modify if T-002 not already covers]
- apps/web/lib/settings/authorization-policies.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/authorization/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/authorization/page.test.tsx [create]
- apps/web/lib/settings/authorization-policies.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given migrations run for a new org, then `org_authorization_policies` has seeded rows for `npd_post_release_edit` (disabled, `requires_new_version=true`) and `technical_product_spec_approval` (enabled when Technical module is enabled, gate `technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1`).
- Given `npd.post_release_edit.enabled` is turned on, when policy lacks authorizer roles/permissions or has `requires_new_version=false`, then V-SET-43 fails and the flag remains off.
- Given a user has `npd.released_product_edit.request` but not `npd.released_product_edit.authorize`, when `canAuthorizePostReleaseEdit()` is called, then it returns false and includes a machine-readable reason.
- Given a user has `technical.product_spec.approve` and matches configured approver role(s), when `canApplyTechnicalApprovalGate()` is called, then it returns true only if policy is enabled and min-approver/segregation settings are satisfiable.
- Given /settings/authorization renders, then it follows UX SET-011b: two policy cards, locked new-version invariant, role multi-selects, min approver controls, validation/misconfigured states, read-only permission strings, and audit-log link.
- Given a policy is saved, then audit_log receives `authorization_policy_update` with old/new JSON diff and reason.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: add unit tests for policy seed, V-SET-43/V-SET-44, `canAuthorizePostReleaseEdit`, and `canApplyTechnicalApprovalGate`; add RTL page test for SET-011b cards and misconfigured state.
- GREEN: add schema/migration, helper functions, Server Actions, and screen.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run apps/web/lib/settings/authorization-policies.test.ts apps/web/app/(admin)/settings/authorization/page.test.tsx`, `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm lint`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement NPD or Technical app workflows in this task; expose policy/helper contracts only.
- Do not allow in-place mutation of approved factory BOM/product-spec versions; `requires_new_version` is locked true for NPD post-release edits.
- Do not grant self-authorization to `npd_manager` by default; distinguish request vs authorize.
- Do not bypass the read-only Rule Registry authoring decision; gate rule code is selected from deployed rules, not edited here.

## Depth patch — auth/settings enforcement blockers (2026-05-03)
This task is the Settings-owned enforcement foundation, not a placeholder. It must implement all of the following within Settings/Auth scope:

### Schema / seed contract
- `org_authorization_policies` must include the PRD columns: `org_id`, `policy_code`, `is_enabled`, `request_permissions`, `authorize_permissions`, `approver_role_codes`, `min_approvers`, `require_segregation_of_duties`, `requires_new_version`, `approval_gate_rule_code`, `settings_json`, `version`, `updated_by`, `updated_at`.
- Constraints: primary key `(org_id, policy_code)`, allowed policy codes only, `min_approvers >= 1`, and `npd_post_release_edit` cannot set `requires_new_version=false`.
- New org seed rows:
  - `npd_post_release_edit`: disabled, request permission `npd.released_product_edit.request`, authorize permission `npd.released_product_edit.authorize`, `requires_new_version=true`, segregation required.
  - `technical_product_spec_approval`: enabled when Technical module is enabled, authorize/approve permission `technical.product_spec.approve`, `approval_gate_rule_code='technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1'`, `min_approvers=1`, segregation required.

### Server Actions / helpers
- Implement read/update/dry-run helpers with typed results: `getAuthorizationPolicies()`, `upsertAuthorizationPolicy()`, `validateAuthorizationPolicyPreflight()`, `canRequestPostReleaseEdit()`, `canAuthorizePostReleaseEdit()`, `canApplyTechnicalApprovalGate()`.
- Helpers must return machine-readable blocker codes for missing policy, disabled policy, missing request permission, missing authorize/approve permission, self-authorization blocked, min approver unsatisfied, wrong/missing gate rule, and `requires_new_version=false`.
- `validateAuthorizationPolicyPreflight()` must be callable from T-020 `setCoreFlag` for V-SET-43/V-SET-44.
- All mutations require `settings.authorization.edit`, increment `version`, write audit action `authorization_policy_update`, and emit a Settings outbox event.

### UI contract — `/settings/authorization` (SET-011b)
- Render two cards: NPD Post-Release Edit Authorization and Technical Product-Spec Approval Gate.
- Show status summary, request/authorize/approve permissions, approver roles, min approvers, segregation-of-duties, requires-new-version lock, gate rule code, and preflight blockers.
- Users with `settings.authorization.view` can read; only `settings.authorization.edit` can save. Unauthorized users see disabled controls and a clear permission message.
- CTA links: Roles & Permissions (`/settings/roles`) for role assignment and Rule Variant Selector (`/settings/tenant/rules`) for read-only gate visibility; neither can bypass policy invariants.

### Tests
- Unit tests must cover schema constraints/seed shape, helper happy paths and blocker codes, V-SET-43/V-SET-44 preflight results, audit/outbox, and segregation-of-duties.
- RTL tests must cover read-only vs editable UI, blocker display, policy save, and links to Roles & Permissions / Rule Variant Selector.

pipeline_inputs.description: Settings-owned per-org authorization policies for NPD post-release edit requests and Technical approval gates.
pipeline_inputs.details: Create policy storage/helpers/actions/screen only. NPD/Technical consume these helpers later; do not edit their modules. Canonical invariant: authorized NPD post-release edit creates a new BOM/product-spec version and must pass Technical approval gate before factory use. Depth patch: must include concrete org_authorization_policies schema constraints, new-org seeds, Server Actions/helpers, V-SET-43/V-SET-44 preflight integration points, SET-011b UI states, audit/outbox, and unit/RTL tests.
scope_files: apps/web/lib/rbac/permissions.enum.ts [modify if T-002 not already covers]
acceptance_criteria: Seeded org_authorization_policies rows for npd_post_release_edit and technical_product_spec_approval.
Request vs authorize vs Technical approve permissions resolved separately.
SET-011b Authorization Policies screen follows UX and audits saves.
Schema constraints prevent invalid policy codes, min_approvers<1, and npd_post_release_edit.requires_new_version=false.
New org seed creates concrete npd_post_release_edit and technical_product_spec_approval rows with technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1.
Server Actions/helpers expose typed preflight blocker codes consumed by T-020 setCoreFlag.
SET-011b supports read-only view, edit/save, blocker summaries, and audit/outbox on save.
Unit and RTL tests cover seed, helper decisions, V-SET-43/V-SET-44 preflight, permissions, audit/outbox, and segregation-of-duties.
test_strategy: RED unit + RTL tests for policy helpers and screen.
RED tests for schema/seed constraints and helper blocker codes before implementation.
RED RTL tests for read-only vs edit mode and blocker display.
GREEN includes T-020-compatible preflight helpers; do not rely on UI-only validation.
risk_red_lines: No in-place mutation path for released factory spec/BOM.
Do not leave org_authorization_policies as unvalidated JSON-only storage; explicit columns/constraints are required.
Do not make technical_product_spec_approval_gate_v1 optional when technical.product_spec_approval.required=true.
Do not allow policy UI to bypass requires_new_version=true or segregation-of-duties.
